raw_response:
I agree with the provided analysis. The formation of a chemical bond with the tooth structure is indeed a shared characteristic between zinc-eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement, even though the exact nature of the bond differs—zinc-eugenol relies more directly on eugenol's interaction while polycarboxylates use acrylic monomers for adhesion. This fundamental similarity makes option B correct without needing additional justification beyond what was already discussed.
